old_claim.note: Paper Making provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Thirteenth-century Italian paper mills made commercial letter production cheaper and more plentiful, but parchment remained an alternative writing surface.

source_shape.source_locator: Material-conditions overview: the transition from parchment to paper and the proliferation of water-powered paper mills in thirteenth-century Italy made letter production economical.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Growing paper production lowered the material cost of letter writing and supported wider commercial correspondence.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source describes an economic scaling effect while also naming parchment as the predecessor medium, so paper accelerates report circulation without being indispensable.

ontology_before: Paper making was modeled as a generic enabling prerequisite even though written reports could use parchment.
ontology_after: Paper making is retained as an accelerator that reduced cost and supported scale without defining the existence of merchant reports.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-16-merchant-market-reports-scope: paper_making is an accelerates edge.
would_reject_if: Evidence shows that the scoped 1265 correspondence could only be produced on paper and not on another writing substrate.
short_reason: Paper made letter production more economical, but parchment remained a viable alternative.
